table_decisions.d3: accepted as proposed; operator_choice and needs_operator rows decided by the delegated judge in the executing tranche with recorded rationale
table_decisions.d5: accepted as proposed; new-domain registrations decided by the delegated judge in wave tranche one with recorded rationale

boundary: delegated class acceptance only; Gate A exact-byte acceptance remains the operator's; every wave tranche is side-by-side reissue and wholesale rejectable

construction_decisions.authority: operator delegation of ADR 0037, recorded in ADR 0048
construction_decisions.rationale: every commitment subject -- ordered digest lists, frontiers, state roots, and graph roots -- is a canonical closed object under the frozen profile, carrying its members explicitly and digested under a per-subject registered domain. Replayability and reviewability are the architecture-stage requirements; incremental-proof machinery may be added later only as a new domain and new field, never by mutating these identities

per_field_decisions.assignment_commit_digest: pinned to the assignment event under odeya-research-event-v1; the append-only event is the truth the commit binds
per_field_decisions.recipient_digest: privacy-preserving byte digest over the exact recipient identifier bytes
per_field_decisions.configuration_digest: byte digest over the retained engine configuration bytes
per_field_decisions.tree_digest: external git tree identity, annotated as such; it does not replace a source-bundle digest
per_field_decisions.weights_or_endpoint_digest: split into weights_digest and endpoint_descriptor_digest; one field must not admit two subjects
per_field_decisions.payload_contract_digest: remains unresolved_blocking per the profile document until a ResearchEvent branch-extraction identity freezes; annotated as unresolved, never inferred
